_comment: RC-0002 — Replication study: same taxpayer, prior year return. Full-salary year with JP Morgan. New features vs RC-0001: high salary (₹19L+), VDA income u/s 115BBH (crypto, 94S TDS), professional tax deduction 16(iii), perquisites ₹6,541, employer TDS computed under old regime with wrong std deduction. Filed within original due date.

head_1_salary.note: Salary ₹18,96,909 + Perquisites ₹6,541 = ₹19,03,450. Employer computed TDS under old regime, applied std deduction ₹50,000 (wrong — AY2025-26 should be ₹75,000) and 80C ₹57,450. Engine should use correct ₹75,000 regardless of employer's TDS computation.

head_5_other_sources.description: Virtual Digital Asset income u/s 115BBH — consideration received from Crypto Platform (anonymized), TDS deducted u/s 94S. Cost of acquisition not available from prefill/AIS data; VDA profit (taxable income) cannot be determined from available evidence.
head_5_other_sources._rule_gap: Section 115BBH not implemented. VDA taxed at 30% flat + cess (no deductions except cost of acquisition). No set-off of VDA losses against any other income. TDS: 1% on consideration (94S).

tds_credits_from_26AS.employer: Crypto Platform (anonymized) — VDA TDS at 1% on ₹1,62,608 consideration

human_expected._comment: New regime. Salary: 19,03,450 − 75,000 (16ia std) − 2,400 (16iii prof tax) = 18,26,050. Other sources: 500 (SAV 496 + DIV 4). VDA income excluded from slab computation — taxed separately at 30%+cess u/s 115BBH; cost of acquisition unknown so total VDA tax cannot be determined. All VDA-dependent fields (total_tax, outcome, refund, demand) set null. taxable_income_new_regime = salary income + other sources = 18,26,050 + 500 = 18,26,550.

notes: HIGH SALARY YEAR. Full-time employment at JP Morgan throughout the year. Key differences from RC-0001: (1) Single employer with large salary — tests high-income slab computation and regime comparison at ₹19L+ income. (2) VDA income (crypto): Neblio Technologies paid ₹1,62,608 consideration under 94S at 1% TDS (₹1,633). Section 115BBH taxes VDA at 30% flat + cess with NO deductions except cost of acquisition. Cost not available from prefill/AIS; profit unknown. Engine rule gap — 115BBH not implemented. (3) Professional tax ₹2,400 u/s 16(iii): deductible under both regimes. Engine does not implement 16iii — engine will over-report taxable income by ₹2,400. (4) Perquisites ₹6,541 included in gross salary — taxable at full value, no exemption claimed. (5) Employer TDS error: employer applied std deduction ₹50,000 (wrong for AY2025-26) and 80C ₹57,450 (invalid under new regime). Employer under-deducted TDS compared to correct new regime computation. Engine will correctly apply ₹75,000 std deduction. (6) No HRA, no Chapter VI-A deductions under new regime. Under old regime, 80C ₹57,450 applies (employer-declared) but taxpayer likely filed under new regime. EXPECTED ENGINE OUTPUT vs HUMAN: taxable_income_new_regime: engine will produce 1828950 (1903450 − 75000 + 500, missing 2400 prof tax) vs human 1826550. Delta: +2400 (16iii professional tax rule gap). VDA TDS credit ₹1,633 (94S) will need to be applied to VDA tax separately. Total TDS ₹3,58,652.

defects_found: D-002: r0022_interest_234abc read tds_credit_from_26AS (scalar key, never populated) instead of summing tds_credits_from_26AS (list). For RC-0001 this was hidden because total_tax=0 so 0>=0.90*0. RC-0002 has total_tax=₹2,48,232 so the wrong tds=0 caused engine to charge ₹19,982 interest (234B) instead of 0. Fix: sum items from tds_credits_from_26AS. 13 GMs regenerated.

D-003: wave3a.py other_total sum used item.get('amount', 0) which returns None when 'amount': null is present in JSON (dict.get default only applies when key is absent, not when value is None). VDA entry with null amount caused TypeError. Fix: use item.get('amount') or 0.
rule_gaps_observed: RG-003: Section 115BBH (VDA income) not implemented — engine computes 0 for VDA income; actual VDA consideration ₹1,62,608, profit unknown. VDA-dependent fields (total_tax, outcome, refund, demand) cannot be verified.
RG-004: Section 16(iii) professional tax deduction not implemented — engine over-reports taxable_income_new_regime by ₹2,400 (engine: ₹18,28,950 vs human: ₹18,26,550).
RG-001: Section 288A rounding not applied — minor delta expected but not observable here since taxable income delta is dominated by RG-004.
RG-002: demand_amount not produced — engine returns None.

delta_analysis.taxable_income_new_regime: +2400 — RG-004: Section 16(iii) professional tax not deducted by engine
delta_analysis.total_tax_new_regime: cannot verify — total_tax includes assumption that VDA income=0 (RG-003); actual VDA tax unknown
delta_analysis.total_interest: 0 — matches human (D-002 fix applied; return filed within due date; TDS ₹3,58,652 > 90% of tax ₹2,48,232)
